intro: Oxytropis immersa, also known as Mountain Sweet Pea or Submerged Locoweed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, leaf-like structures. The plant is used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis and respiratory issues.
